canonical_definition: Within the provided literature, ethical alignment is characterized as a latent model tendency to generate responses that conform to professional ethical standards, particularly in service contexts. As defined in "Dynamic Collaboration of Multi-Language Models based on Minimal Complete Semantic Units," this involves avoiding harmful, misleading, or inappropriate content. The stated objective is to produce "professional and ethical responses" that align with both ethical standards and professional goals. The provided data does not specify any particular instruments or tasks used to operationalize or measure this construct.
papers: Dynamic Collaboration of Multi-Language Models based on Minimal Complete Semantic Units
EasyRec: Simple yet Effective Language Models for Recommendation
Mind the Inclusivity Gap: Multilingual Gender-Neutral Translation Evaluation with mGeNTE

definitions.text: The latent tendency of the model to generate responses that conform to professional ethical standards and avoid harmful, misleading, or inappropriate content in service contexts.
definitions.paper: Dynamic Collaboration of Multi-Language Models based on Minimal Complete Semantic Units
